chapter_title.en: Chapter on Performing Hajj Al-tamattu‘ on Behalf of one's Father

text: 2932 - رَوَى جَعْفَرُ بْنُ بَشِيرٍ عَنِ اَلْعَلاَءِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ يَحُجُّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ أَ يَتَمَتَّعُ قَالَ «نَعَمْ اَلْمُتْعَةُ لَهُ وَ اَلْحَجُّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2932 - Ja'far ibn Bashir narrated from Al-'Ala', from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja'far (as), who said:
I asked him about a man who performs Hajj on behalf of his father. Should he perform Tamattu‘ (Umrah and Hajj combined)?
Imam (as) said: "Yes, the Tamattu‘ is for him, and the Hajj is on behalf of his father."
